questions.question_text: Answer the following questions CO Mapping a State Bragg’s law. What information is required to index an unknown XRD pattern? 2.5 b What is the function of a condenser lens in an optical microscope? 2.5 c What is the basic principle of Rutherford backscattering spectroscopy ? Which type of particles are used in RBS and why? 2.5 d What are the possible outcomes during the inelastic scattering process of an electron with an atom in a material? 2.5 e Differentiate between positive and negative magnetoresistance. 2.5 f Explain the significance of relaxation time in NMR Spectroscopy. 2.5 g How is the bandgap of a material estimated using UV-Visible Spectroscopy? 2.5 h Differentiate between Stokes and anti-Stokes lines in Raman spectra. 2.5

An X-ray diffraction pattern of a nanomaterial with a cubic crystal structure shows a strong diffraction peak at an angle of 2θ = 36.5°, corresponding to the plane, using Cu Kα radiation . Using Bragg's Law, calculate the interplanar spacing and also determine the lattice constant of the material. If the full width at half maximum of the peak is 0.5°, calculate the crystallite size and the dislocation density of the material. 10
Explain the principle of operation of RHEED with the help of a suitable diagram. Is RHEED better than LEED? Justify. 10
Draw the schematic ray diagrams of the various imaging modes of a SEM. Differentiate between SEM and FESEM. 10

Using suitable schematic diagrams, explain the principle and tip nature of Scanning Probe Microscopy. Why do we use a sharp tip in SPM? What characterization techniques are based upon the SPM principle? 10

Explain the principle of the Hall effect in semiconductors. Derive the expression for the Hall coefficient and discuss its significance. In a Hall effect experiment, a sample of thickness 0.5 mm carries a current of 5 mA. A magnetic field of 0.8 T is applied perpendicular to the sample surface. A Hall voltage of 3 mV is measured across the width of the sample. Calculate the Hall coefficient and the carrier concentration. Also, determine the type of charge carriers. 10
Explain Thermogravimetric analysis in terms of processes that cause weight gain in TG curves. Describe a few applications of TGA. 10
Explain the basic principle of UV-Vis spectroscopy. Using suitable diagrams, describe the two basic conditions that must be satisfied for absorption to occur. 10
What do you mean by magnetoresistance? How is GMR different from normal MR? Diagrammatically explain the two principal geometries of the GMR effect. 10
